Purchase.title: Purchase
Purchase.description: Represents a purchase transaction of gas cylinders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the purchase transaction.

Sale.title: Sale
Sale.description: Represents a sale transaction of gas cylinders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the sale transaction.

saleType.description: Type of sale (Counter Sale, Delivery Boy Sale).

deliveryBoyId.description: Reference to DeliveryBoy. (Relationship: DeliveryBoy 1:N Sale). Nullable if saleType is Counter Sale.

expenseType.description: Type of expense (e.g., Daily Wages, Diesel, Electricity Bill, Chit, Custom).

id.description: Unique identifier for the delivery boy.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Only the authenticated user can access their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores purchase transactions.  Accessible to authorized agency personnel.

params.name: purchaseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the purchase transaction.

definition.description: Stores sales transactions, including counter sales and delivery boy sales. Accessible to authorized agency personnel. Includes 'deliveryBoyId' for associating sales with delivery boys.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the sale transaction.

definition.description: Stores expense records. Accessible to authorized agency personnel.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the expense.

definition.description: Stores delivery boy information.  Accessible to authorized agency personnel.

params.name: deliveryBoyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the delivery boy.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to create an E-Ledger for Varnika Indane Gramin Vitarak, focusing on daily tracking of purchases, sales (counter and delivery boy), and expenses. The structure prioritizes Authorization Independence by avoiding `get()` calls in security rules. Data is segregated to maintain homogeneous security postures and follows consistent access modeling for private and collaborative data.

**Authorization Independence:** There's no reliance on parent document data for child document access. All necessary authorization data (e.g., delivery boy ID for sales) is contained within the document itself, removing the need for `get()` calls in security rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   Secure `list` operations are supported via structural segregation. Each top-level collection (`purchases`, `sales`, `expenses`, `delivery_boys`) has a defined and consistent security posture, allowing rules to be applied effectively without filtering.
*   Path-based ownership is used for the `users/{userId}` collection, ensuring that only the authenticated user can access their profile data.
